maintenance_note: Disney publishes the official party calendar in spring (Halloween) and summer (Christmas) each year. Replace the `dates` arrays with the actual published schedule when Disney announces (~10 min of manual work per season). Look for 'After Hours' or 'Special Events' at the source URL above. Flip each party's dates_status to verified_from_disney_calendar once updated.

MNSSHP.full_name: Mickey's Not So Scary Halloween Party
MNSSHP.dates_caveat: Verified from disneyworld.com/events on 2026-05-11. 38 party nights across August-October.

crowd_effects.reason_daytime_lighter: AP holders and locals avoid days where the park closes early at 6pm for non-party guests; family/leisure guests skew toward other parks
crowd_effects.evening_party_crowds: moderate with capped attendance (~50% of daytime capacity)
crowd_effects.evening_wait_times: often SHORTER than typical daytime waits during the party itself

non_party_ticket_implications: Park closes at 18:00 for guests without party tickets — plan to be out (or moved to another park) by then
Trick-or-treating, character meets in costume, and special parades/fireworks are party-ticket-exclusive
If user mentions they're going on a party day WITHOUT a party ticket: warn about the 6pm closure upfront and suggest moving evening rides to mid-day

MVMCP.dates_caveat: Replace with verified 2026 dates from disneyworld.com/events. Disney usually announces in Apr-May.

crowd_effects.reason_daytime_lighter: Same as MNSSHP — 6pm closure drives locals away from MK on these days
crowd_effects.evening_wait_times: often shorter than typical daytime; in December the surrounding non-party days (holiday tourists) are MUCH busier than party days

non_party_ticket_implications: Park closes at 18:00 for guests without party tickets
Special holiday parade (Mickey's Once Upon a Christmastime Parade), fireworks, character meets in holiday costumes, and snow on Main Street are party-ticket-exclusive

December non-party days are some of the highest-crowd days of the year — if user has any flexibility, party days can be a better choice for actually riding things even without a party ticket (use the daytime, leave by 6pm)
